name: Amiqus
notes: Slow churn as they have reduced dev resource. Asked for reduced contract value, which has been shared 2nd March.

name: Attensi
notes:
They are still discussing our plan internally, and should be making a decision by the end of this week (w/c 09/03). CM to book a call w/c 16th. 

.Positive relationship, sentiment is OK. Disappointed we're moving away from unification, as this is what they bought. 

We're fairly embedded. 8 linked accounts across four connectors. 

Attensi are currently testing Falcon to inform their decision. They have a capable development team and a skilled CTO. 

There's also a potential expansion of their use case into documents.

name: Birdie
notes: 2 auth configs. 1 connector, gong. No API requests.

name: Bridge & Co
notes:
Ask Elena. Facing 6 API requests, errors. CM reach out.

Elena's brother in law's company in Brazil - interesting for us to get intros to ENT clients and learn about their interoperability needs

name: CandidateHub
notes: Lever config done but not connected. CM msg 19/03

name: Drata
notes:
Get renewal signed as top priority, flat renewal

Falcon: Demo complete on Agentic Architecture. Exploring POC for compliance automation. 

Policies/Docs of interest, no commit yet

Continue with IAM linked account expansion and LMS expansion.

name: Episode 1
notes: CHECK: 309 requests, Affinity & Mailchimp set up

name: Ficell
notes: No activity, Pylon tickets, Cm chase 19/03.

name: Fuse Universal
notes:
Meeting booked 7th April. Goal - get feedback on their approach for Falcon (agentic use case). Start to schedule in a migration window. 

_______

Ongoing issue with Go1 connector. 

Fuse understands Falcon capabilities — can build connectors faster, but still need internal workflow development, especially for Agent/Managed RAG.  

Architectural decision needed — whether to:                                                                             
    - Commit dev resources to N8N + StackOne integration                                                                     
    - Focus on current use case only                                                                                         
    - Plan transition away from StackOne

name: G-P
notes:
CM: Reached out to Kelsey & Mithun 18th March 
__

Looks as though Gia team are trialling other connectors. GP is split into two sections. Gia (agentic, now in prod with 4 accounts), using Documents, and EOR (18 linked accounts, 5 live connectors).

We have a strong relationship with Mithun at EOR, who is excited to see a Falcon demo. This has been delayed due to their internal resource as well as ours. The EOR branch has exciting plans to expand their use cases, whether it's for time off into ATS connectors or into accounting connectors. Therefore, our toolkit would be helpful for them to expand without relying on StackOne.

name: Gaia (Socially Recruited)
notes: Renewal upcoming, charlie has chased. Making a decision.

name: Getabstract
notes: Testing Falcon to inform their decision

name: Getflip
notes:
Next step: 

- Compile Flip agentic contract 
______

Completed a PoC of Falcon. Gave product feedback which we've responded to and implemented. 

Also recently deployed Payslip download for Workday / ADP.

name: GoGlobal
notes:
CM check in on progress 18th March

Strong partner. CTO Marcus committed "100% StackOne for platform integrations." GG goal is 100% agentic by end of year. Successfully demoed unification for UKGReady Feb 27. 
Currently building this out. Also wants Sapien: propose to move to usage based pricing at renewal in April.

name: GoodHabitz
notes: Wants to chat commercials for Falcon.

name: Halo Benefits Ltd
notes: Xero configured but not linked. CM msg 19/03.

name: Hownow Technologies Pvt Ltd
notes: 26k requests. CM / Richard to reach out for minimum spend: email drafted.

name: Humaans
notes:
Testing MCP connectors - CM to arrange facetime, training, goal capturing. Mini-workshop with Sean/Karolis/Angus. Msged on 18th.

Strategic customer, who are also very low touch. 30 linked accounts across 4 or 5 HRIS/ATS.

Prime candidate to move to Falcon. 

We discussed managed RAG, but they've now gone with a different provider for this. 

Main issue we face is getting FaceTime with their CTO or commitment to test out Falcon. The CTO tried testing it out but did not make any progress.

name: Incubane
notes: Romain's recommendation - Elena requested warm intros to managing partners from https://www.linkedin.com/in/oliver-david-jackson/

name: Instructure
notes: We've demoed Falcon and shared pricing. Charlie reached out to Aaron 10.03 to schedule a meeting.

name: IRIS / Staffology / CascadeHR
notes:
No response to multiple outreach.

____

Informed about new engine but no technical deep dive yet. Evaluating contract options. Need detailed technical session to clarify capabilities, timelines, and use cases.

Phase 1 work deprioritised.

name: Jointflows
notes: Salesforce config done, but no linked account. Cm msg 19/03.

name: Kinfolk
notes:
Meeting with Jeet & Kim to outline what is possible on Falcon now and what isn't.

______

Key issues: Notion API disconnects, need better documentation of connector limitations.

name: Localyze
notes:
We pitched Falcon w/c 9th March. Interested in the integration builder and potentially agentic capabilities.

-------

Currently have 12 linked accounts.

David, Snr PM has been interested in StackOne disfacing merge at Boundless. Given the pivot, this was put off. 

They have specific connector requirements which we can now meet using the builder, such as Rippling. 

Partnership opportunity with Gusto, who are potentially a partner or customer of theirs. 

Demo booked for w/c 10th of March.

name: Mind Tools for Business
notes: Slow churn confirmed. CM to share new reduced contract.

name: Mindset.ai
notes:
CM reached out to Maddie & Jack on Slack on 18th March to understand where they are at. 
_____

Stack One integration ON PAUSE - team focused on other priorities. 

Plan: build relationship with Maddie and Jack, what are their objectives with StackOne, what are other priorities.

JC (Jonathan) was working on Teams connector but unclear on progress. Biweekly meetings suspended until work resumes.

name: O'Reilly
notes:
They are just in the process of getting a Skillsoft sandbox and API keys, to test Falcon. 

Then, Charlie to discuss next steps with Pascal/Simon, offering managed service. 

________

Demo complete, as well as a detailed run-through of how to build a specific connector on Falcon (Skillsoft), on the 3rd of March. 

Senitment is poor, but justified. They have had a poor experience since joining, due to the pivot, as well as other issues:

- Cornerstone not supported on Unified or Falcon
- Other connectors not supported for their use case on Falcon, as we do not support batch upload
- They also faced issues with sandboxes

name: PENTEX, LLC DBA BUDDYS HOME FURNISHINGS
Pinpoint
notes:
Gmail, erroring.

______

uses Kombo, Romain wasted a lot of time on him. Will give me 3 months updates and only doing effort if he wants to change.

name: Popp
notes:
17th March - built Dayforce connector with Ilyes.

________

Suspect that Kombo are going after Popp. Currently paying us 97k. Speaking with Sam w/c 16th. 

He's asked about DayForce, Mercury, Rippling, all of which will be able to be unlocked by Falcon. Also meeting Ilyes to show how to add unification. Pair session to build a new connector. Show migration plan. 

They need to wait for Webhooks.

They have a high volume of unique connectors enabled and linked accounts. 

They have faced significant issues, however, mainly around webhooks.

name: Proppl
notes: Not informed yet, as StackOne project has been put on hold due to internal resource

name: Reachdesk
notes: Testing Falcon to inform their decision

name: Screenloop
notes: Renewal end of March. Addendum sent.

name: Socialtalent
notes:
Negative feedback on Falcon from their engineering team. Tristam is going to collect the feedback and share it with us so we can address this. 

Meeting with Lead Dev 5th March to discuss Falcon following some testing from their team. Otherwise, very positive sentiment following the ATS implementation. Slow adoption of LMS remains.

name: Spottedzebra
notes: Churned - Charlie to check this, as apparently they're also using Kombo.

name: StaffCircle
notes: Informed and Making a Decision also requested a demo.

name: SuperOrgs
notes:
CM msg 19/03. 

Auth configured, no connectors or API requests. Bamboo and Gusto. Discussion on Pylon.

name: Synthesia
notes: Workshop on HRIS connectors successful. Hub embedded. Positive sentiment. Working through snagging list - Max compiling bug/roadmap requests. Key asks: semantic versioning for connectors, dashboard improvements.

name: UJJI
notes:
Demo of Falcon on Friday, led by Alex. 

Still in implementation, embedding file picker.

name: Vizzy
notes:
Demo booked Monday 16th, with Chris/Joe. Offer managed service (?)

Vizzy has been quiet, as of late (in terms of support/unified growth). Joe, CPO, has asked to see a demo - in the process of organizing this. Suggest we put them on a discounted managed service offer where we migrate for them, and then they can build connectors themselves.

name: WorkPro
notes: Informed that making a decision is not a priority for us to migrate this customer
